_meta.name: lava-design-tokens
_meta.note: Hand-synced with the live Swift sources @ v1.0.0, read directly so the spec is truthful. LavaStyle/LavaTypography stay the live source for iOS; the portable glyph-size scale lives in LavaIconSize (LavaSecCore) so the widget extension can share it. No automated drift-guard yet (see deferred); the source-string tests in LavaDesignTokensSourceTests pin the contract for now.
_meta.deferred: When Android becomes a real second consumer: (a) write generate-design-tokens.swift to emit a delimited // BEGIN generated ... // END generated region of LavaTokens.swift AND a Compose lavaLightColors()/lavaDarkColors() + spacing/shape theme from this spec; (b) add a CI drift-guard (re-run generator -> fail on diff). Both gated on this version.

primaryText.android: map to the platform on-surface/secondary/tertiary text color

secondaryText.android: map to the platform on-surface/secondary/tertiary text color

tertiaryText.android: map to the platform on-surface/secondary/tertiary text color

iconSizes._note: SF Symbol glyph point sizes fed to .font(.system(size:)). Live source: LavaIconSize (LavaSecCore). One value per role; near-duplicate hand-tuned sizes were reconciled (see _reconciled). Android maps these to dp on the same role table.

_reconciled.hero: 42/44/46/48 -> 44 (one symbol, six call sites, four sizes)
_reconciled.heroResult: 54/58 -> 56 (success check + failure triangle share one slot)

typography._note: Body/label text uses the platform's semantic Dynamic Type styles directly (iOS: .headline/.subheadline/.body/...) so it tracks the user's text-size setting. Only genuinely fixed display faces are tokenized here. Live source: LavaTypography (LavaTokens.swift).

metricNumeral.usage: overview metric block headline value; pair with monospacedDigit
metricNumeral.android: map to a rounded bold display style at ~42sp with tabular figures

tiers._note: LavaTier depth vocabulary (calm=Floor, celebratory=Window, technical=Workshop). accent resolves to a color role.
